title: baccharis glutinosa var. glutinosa
intro: Baccharis glutinosa var. glutinosa, also known as the sticky groundsel or sticky baccharis, is a shrub-like plant native to South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and alleviate congestion, making it a popular herbal remedy in folk medicine. Baccharis glutinosa var. glutinosa can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
